input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML approach** — use `AppThemeBinding` to specify values for `Light` and `Dark`:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">
        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="24"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#F0F0F0}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#333333, Dark=#CCCCCC}" />

        <Button Text="Click Me"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

The framework detects the OS theme automatically and picks the matching value. When the user toggles system dark mode, the UI updates live.

---

**C# alternative** — use `SetAppTheme` or `SetAppThemeColor`:

```csharp
// In your page constructor or handler:
var page = new ContentPage();
page.SetAppThemeColor(ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Colors.White, Colors.Parse("#1E1E1E"));

var label = new Label { Text = "Welcome", FontSize = 24 };
label.SetAppThemeColor(Label.TextColorProperty,
    Colors.Black, Colors.Parse("#F0F0F0"));

var button = new Button { Text = "Click Me" };
button.SetAppThemeColor(Button.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Colors.Parse("#6200EE"), Colors.Parse("#BB86FC"));
button.SetAppThemeColor(Button.TextColorProperty,
    Colors.White, Colors.Black);
```

For non-color properties, use the generic `SetAppTheme<T>`:

```csharp
image.SetAppTheme<FileImageSource>(
    Image.SourceProperty, "light_logo.png", "dark_logo.png");
```

---

**Key points:**
- No manual detection code needed — MAUI subscribes to `Application.RequestedThemeChanged` internally.
- Works with resources too: define `Light`/`Dark` colors in `ResourceDictionary` and reference them via `{StaticResource}` inside `AppThemeBinding`.
- To force a theme (ignoring system), set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark`.

This is a knowledge/explanation question, not a code modification task on an existing file. I'll provide a concise but complete answer.
